title: Senior Software Engineer
summary: Owning the identity platform post-acquisition — SCIM provisioning, unified OIDC auth, single logout, per-tenant schema isolation, and a resilient CPS publish queue with exponential backoff retries. Serving thousands of auth requests/min across Workday and Evisort.

items.title: Built SCIM API endpoints for Workday-Evisort user provisioning
bullets: Delivered the first Workday-Evisort integration post-acquisition (CRUD + Restore) in the identity service.
Implemented dynamic model creation for provisioning flexibility across Workday and the legacy identity provider.
Led GA hardening: deterministic role mapping ("highest role wins"), SCIM import failure fixes, and Workday identifier handling for migration stability.

items.title: Improved identity and document platform services for Workday user provisioning
bullets: Established a dedicated internal service-to-service channel (HTTP Signature Auth, dedicated port) for secure provisioning.
Fixed 2 critical IDOR vulnerabilities in document version endpoints — changed cross-tenant access from 403 to 404.
Built an async user creation task for post-SCIM user provisioning with retry logic and outbox events.

items.title: Implemented unified authentication via OIDC
bullets: Built a high-throughput token exchange endpoint handling tens of thousands of requests per minute.
Designed OIDC callback handling across identity and auth services (JWT generation, token verification, identifier resolution).
Integrated cookie-based session management and state-based redirection for deep linking.

items.title: Designed end-to-end unified logout between Evisort and Workday
bullets: Implemented frontchannel (Evisort-initiated) and backchannel (Workday-initiated) Single Logout flows.
Built dynamic JWT logout token validation using internal certificate authority discovery.
Demoed the complete logout flow to the engineering team and company-wide.

items.title: Owned the Super Admin Toggles EPIC for migration-safe identity architecture
bullets: Drove full technical design, ticket decomposition, and implementation across 4 services and the UI.
Introduced per-client toggles (user_management, authentication_provider) to decouple tenant mapping from login enforcement.
Designed backward-compatible data migration with feature flag controls for safe rollout.

items.title: Architecting per-client PostgreSQL schema isolation for the auth gateway
bullets: Replacing shared public schema with per-tenant isolation to reduce cross-tenant blast radius.
Implementing dual-write architecture with feature-flagged reads for zero-downtime migration.

items.title: Built CPS Publish Queue with retries and exponential backoff
bullets: Designed a DB-backed task queue so all CPS publish operations (REGISTER, DEREGISTER, UPDATE, RECREATE) are enqueued asynchronously and processed by a dedicated worker.
Implemented exponential backoff retries (base 30s, max 600s, 6 retries) — CPS publishes survive 12+ hours of downtime during quarterly hybrid patching.
Built atomic RECREATE (deregister old → register new) with partial-failure recovery and snapshot-based DEREGISTER resilience.
Reduced Sentry alerts from ~50 per task to max 6; created a datapatch to re-enqueue previously failed tasks.

items.title: Conducted live engineering demos and led design reviews
bullets: Presented SCIM, UPW, and unified authentication at Evisort Tech Demo and Eng Syncs.
Documented workflows and recorded sessions to support team onboarding.

items.title: Owned on-call rotations and incident response
bullets: Reviewed and documented 5+ past incidents (SCIM sync failures, token verification issues).
Built a Confluence debugging playbook and mapped service ownership across 4+ platform services.

items.title: Buddied 2 new hires; participated in 14 interview loops (3 successful hires)
bullets: Created a repeatable onboarding reference guide.
Provided feedback to improve onboarding documentation and materials.

items.title: Led Operational Readiness Reviews (ORR) for the auth gateway
bullets: Authored ORR documentation aligned with Workday reliability standards.
Enabled Prometheus-based observability and alerting; extended instrumentation to the NestJS gateway.
Raised authentication test coverage to 80%+.

items.title: Improved platform stability and scalability across identity and document platform services
bullets: Resolved key integration bugs and profiled the high-traffic token exchange endpoint.
Refactored core components for maintainability and increased test coverage.

items.title: Drove cross-service integration resilience
bullets: Built robust SSO token validation using internal CA certificate discovery.
Enabled auth gateway observability via /ping and /health endpoints.
Standardized metadata propagation across platform services and UI.
Refactored auth library naming conventions across Python and TypeScript codebases.

title: Software Engineer
summary: Built Voice AI platform end-to-end — media microservices, SIP APIs, and Kafka schema service. Cut latency 35% and scaled concurrent calls to 700.

items.title: Rebuilt Connector Service — Reduced data event size by 40% and latency by 35%.
bullets: Identified bottlenecks in inter-service communication.
Designed an optimized event pipeline with Redis-backed brand configuration lookups.

items.title: Redesigned Media Service into microservices — Increased concurrent calls by 50% (450 to 700).
bullets: Decomposed monolithic service into TTS, STT, and media microservices.
Defined and implemented the observability strategy (metrics, monitoring, alerting).

items.title: Owned Audio File Management Service end-to-end — Drove 25% increase in user engagement.
bullets: Architected storage and retrieval layer for audio blobs and Azure client keys using GCS and Redis.
Integrated Azure Text-to-Speech for multi-language support via SSML.

items.title: Proposed and drove adoption of Centralized Schema Service — Reduced integration overhead by 40% and debugging time by 25%.
bullets: Identified fragmented Kafka schema management as a cross-team pain point.
Designed the service to enforce schema evolution and consistency.
Authored internal documentation and onboarding guides adopted across teams.

items.title: Designed and delivered SIP Connection APIs — Increased customer retention by 20%.
bullets: Built SIP connection support (IP, FQDN, Credentials) using Telnyx SDKs, Java, and Micronaut with Kafka.

items.title: Optimized shared utility libraries — Improved code efficiency by 22% across internal services.
bullets: Tools: Redis, Log4j, GCS, Firestore.

items.title: Built automated OOM diagnostics — Reduced debugging time by 25%.
bullets: Automated thread and heap dump backups to GCP buckets during Out-of-Memory incidents.

items.title: Secured transaction logs and reduced on-call burden — Cut on-call debugging time by 4 hours weekly.
bullets: Built bash tooling for log retrieval from Kubernetes pods and GCP Logs Explorer.

items.title: Drove data security improvements
bullets: Implemented data redaction for NPIs, auth tokens, and credentials across GCP logs and Kubernetes pods.
Eliminated sensitive data exposure in production logs, enabling compliance with enterprise data handling requirements.

items.title: Raised test coverage from 36% to 87% — Cut testing costs by 20%.
bullets: Used JUnit and JMeter; established testing standards for the team.

items.title: Led Inbound Call Automation Bots initiative
bullets: Established a fully operational system for weekly performance runs.
Built the initial proof of concept and coordinated cross-team end-to-end testing.
Delivered knowledge transfer to ensure long-term ownership.

title: Software Engineer

sections.intro: Architected and developed APIs for Input & Output Handler Services on the AIVA (Artificial Intelligence Virtual Assistant) team.

bullets: Utilized AWS services (API Gateway, Lambda, S3, DynamoDB, SSM).

items.title: Designed OCR data extraction microservices
bullets: Increased data accuracy from 80% to 89% and improved performance by 25%.

items.title: Led greenfield service development
bullets: Built resilient services and orchestrated infrastructure stacks using Python, CloudFormation, and S3.

items.title: Resolved provisioning and build pipeline failures
bullets: Reduced testing load times and lowered AWS costs across environments.

items.title: Automated transaction anomaly recovery using Python and Boto3.
bullets: Eliminated 80% of manual retry attempts caused by code defects or AWS outages.

title: Research Scholar
summary: Published two research papers on cloud security steganography and NLP-based clinical decision support systems.

sections.intro: CSE Department under Dr. Rachna Jain — Published two research papers.

items.title: Enhance Data Security in Cloud Using Steganography
bullets: Studied Cloud Service Providers vs Internet Service Providers based on cost, speed, scalability, and performance.
Reviewed techniques of steganography (LSB) and digital watermarking (DFT) in spatial and frequency domain.
In-depth study on Symmetric Key Encryption (DES, AES) and Asymmetric Key Encryption (RSA).
Proposed a model for Image Steganography using LSB, mapping every bit of data and encryption key in RGB pixels.

items.title: Medical Application Using NLP Over Cloud
bullets: Knowledge acquisition on NLP systems and open source libraries (NLTK, Polyglot, Pattern).
Studied Morphological Processing, Syntax, Semantic and Pragmatic Analysis.
Comparative study on public, private, and hybrid clouds based on reliability, cost, and scalability.
Proposed a computerized Clinical Decision Support (CDS) System using cloud services.

title: Consulting & Software Intern
summary: Built a full-stack web app with Flask and MongoDB — drove 45% sales increase and 30% faster query performance via ETL optimization.

items.title: Full stack API-driven web application — Increased sales by 45% and reduced data storage inventory by 38%.
bullets: Implemented Agile process across teams, improved customer interaction and sprint feedback.
Developed RESTful APIs using Python with Flask, JWT Session Management for security.
Performed ETL on legacy database, improved query performance by 30%.
Interacted with the DB using MongoDB Alchemy (NoSQL), improving flexibility and providing Object-Oriented structure.

Worked on app UI, deep functionality, and third-party SDKs.
